themes.exampleQuote: I've been to Belgium several times and this place has waffles that are just as good as what you could get in Liège.
I'm an ice cream snob and this is some of the best ice cream I've ever had.
The comment along the lines of 'I'm not going to waste any more ice cream on you all,' delivered in a rude tone, made us feel uncomfortable.
The owner is extremely friendly and helped make the experience that much better.
Add that delicious waffle to it and you go into a brand new realm of delicious.

notable_quote: I've been to Belgium several times and this place has waffles that are just as good as what you could get in Liège.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers are consistent and emphatic about the product — the ice cream and Liège waffles are genuinely exceptional — but a recurring thread around sample-tasting interactions introduces a service friction that surfaces across more than one visit.
quarter_narrative: Twenty-nine Pittsburgh creators filmed Treat this quarter and racked up 1.3 million plays on TikTok — and Treat itself has been silent on Instagram for 199 days, posting nothing while the city shoots content inside its shop. That gap defines the Spring 2026 story here: a product compelling enough to drive unsolicited six-figure video coverage, paired with an owned channel that has gone completely dark. The Pittsburgh Sweets leader this issue is Page's, whose momentum is being driven by sustained social output; Treat sits at seventh in that family despite a stronger-than-median creator signal and 623 Google reviews at 98% five-star. Closing the Instagram gap — and resolving the service-consistency thread before it compounds — is what moves this business out of the Ones to Watch tier.
tldr_read: A million TikTok plays with zero posts in six months, a stellar product signal, and one recurring service friction: Ones to Watch, #3 in Pittsburgh Ice Cream.
tldr_meaning: The TikTok and review axes are already performing at or above family medians; the Instagram dormancy is the single variable suppressing the overall signal, and the service sentiment thread is the only review axis with downward pressure.

diagnosis_pullquote.line: The City Has Watched 1.3 Million TikTok Plays Here — The Shop Has Posted Nothing In Six Months

playbook.headline: Restart Instagram before the gap compounds
playbook.action: Account has been silent 199 days; one post per week featuring the waffle-and-ice-cream combo stops the signal erosion immediately.

playbook.headline: Post a visible sample policy at the counter
playbook.action: Two reviews cite the same tasting-limit friction; a clear posted limit removes ambiguity and eliminates the recurring negative thread before it becomes a pattern in the star rating.

playbook.headline: Pull the 29 creators into the feed
playbook.action: Twenty-nine creators filmed here this quarter for 1.3M plays; reposting one creator video monthly converts passive TikTok reach into owned Instagram content with no production cost.
